step.text: Upload innovation description and supporting documents
step.name: Step 4

mainEntity.name: What is the tax benefit under Startup India?

acceptedAnswer.text: Section 80-IAC: 100% deduction of profits for 3 consecutive years out of the first 10 years from incorporation. Available only to companies (not LLPs) with turnover under ₹100 crore. Requires inter-ministerial board certificate.
Yes. LLPs can get DPIIT recognition. However, the Section 80-IAC tax exemption applies only to companies, not LLPs.

mainEntity.name: How long does DPIIT registration take?

acceptedAnswer.text: Online application via startupindia.gov.in. DPIIT recognition: typically 2-5 working days. Section 80-IAC certification: additional process through inter-ministerial board, 30-60 days.

mainEntity.name: What qualifies as a startup under DPIIT?

acceptedAnswer.text: Entity incorporated/registered in India for less than 10 years. Turnover less than ₹100 crore in any financial year. Working towards innovation, development, or improvement of products/processes/services. Not formed by splitting or reconstruction.

mainEntity.name: What benefits does DPIIT recognition provide?

acceptedAnswer.text: Self-certification for labour and environment laws (3 years). Tax exemption under Section 80-IAC (3 of 10 years). Fast-track patent examination (80% rebate on patent filing). Easy winding up under IBC (90 days). Fund of Funds eligibility.

mainEntity.name: How to apply for DPIIT startup recognition?

acceptedAnswer.text: Register on startupindia.gov.in. Provide: incorporation certificate, brief description of innovation, recommendation letter or patent/support. Approval: 2-3 working days for most applications.

mainEntity.name: Is DPIIT recognition needed for fundraising?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not mandatory for fundraising. But provides: angel tax exemption (for old cases), labour law self-certification, patent fast-track, and credibility with investors. Recommended for all startups.

mainEntity.name: Can an LLP get DPIIT startup recognition?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Both Pvt Ltd and LLP can get DPIIT recognition. However, LLPs cannot issue equity shares, so fundraising advantages are limited to Pvt Ltd. Partnership firms can also apply.
